meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve Debugging Woes
content.seo_description: Stop guessing why your code fails. Learn how to implement analytics to track runtime errors and user behavior, turning bugs into actionable insights.
content.answer_block: Debugging is impossible without observability. By integrating event-based analytics, you transform silent failures into tracked data points. This allows you to identify exactly where your logic breaks, when it happens, and why. Stop flying blind; implement telemetry to gain total visibility into your application's real-world behavior and state.
content.intro:
You’ve been there: the cursor blinks, the console is empty, and your app behaves like a ghost in the machine. You know something is broken, but you don't know where. This isn't a failure of your intelligence; it’s a failure of your feedback loop. When you lack visibility into your code’s execution, you are effectively trying to perform surgery in the dark.

Today, we stop the guessing game. We are moving from 'hoping it works' to 'knowing it works' by implementing professional-grade analytics. This is the exact transition that separates hobbyist coders from software engineers. Let’s build a system that tells you exactly what went wrong before you even realize it did.

steps.title: Define Your Critical Path Events
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the three most important actions a user takes in your app. Tracking these provides the 'happy path' baseline.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project structure and suggest a schema for tracking user interaction events on my critical path.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'map out user flow and suggest event tracking points'

steps.title: Initialize Analytics Provider
steps.description: Integrate a lightweight provider to collect telemetry without bloating your app's load time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean wrapper for an analytics provider that includes error logging and event tracking methods.

steps.title: Implement Global Error Boundaries
steps.description: Capture unhandled exceptions automatically. This ensures you never miss a crash, even if it happens on a user's device.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a global error handler that catches exceptions and sends them to my analytics dashboard with stack traces.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'implement global error boundary for logging'

steps.title: Inject Contextual Metadata
steps.description: Raw errors are useless without context. Attach user IDs, session state, and input parameters to your logs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Modify my tracking function to automatically inject session state and user context into every logged event.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'add metadata injection to analytics logger'

steps.title: Verification and Smoke Testing
steps.description: Verify your pipeline by triggering an intentional error to ensure the data reaches your dashboard.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test script that triggers a simulated exception and verifies the analytics event is fired correctly.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --test 'verify analytics event firing on error'

pro_tips: Always log the 'previous state' before an error occurs; the sequence of events is often more valuable than the error itself.
Use sampling for high-frequency events to avoid hitting rate limits and incurring unnecessary infrastructure costs.
Never log PII (Personally Identifiable Information) directly; hash user IDs to maintain privacy while keeping data traceable.
Set up automated alerts for 'spike' errors; if an error rate jumps by 5%, your Slack should ping immediately.
Treat your analytics dashboard as a living document; if you aren't looking at a chart, delete the code that generates it.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, spend 15 minutes a day reviewing your error logs. Look for patterns—do errors happen on specific browsers? During certain user actions? Use these insights to prioritize your next feature build.
content.conversion_block: You now have the visibility to master your codebase and build with the confidence of a lead engineer. Don't let bugs hinder your potential; use these tools to scale your skills rapidly. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
